title: Solutions Architect
description:
Job Scope: 
In this exciting Pre-Sales role, you will work between Evertz clients, Account Managers, R&D Engineers, Project Managers, and Executive Management to completely architect, design and document the requirements for Evertz end-to-end Software Defined Video Networking (SDVN) solutions for broadcast and media facilities.

Responsibilities:

  • Work collaboratively with the account management team to define and document the scope of IP Broadcasting systems based on Evertz technologies
  • Assist customers and sales staff with selecting the right Evertz product/technology to meet end application needs
  • Understand competitive offerings and position Evertz solutions to take maximum advantage of key differentiators and be able to present that confidently to our customers
  • Refine system designs and provide accompanying documentation/requirements captures to help drive development of final specifications and contract terms
  • Take the lead on proposal creation and refinement
  • Collaborate with R&D on system designs and compliance documentation for RFI/RFP responses
  • Identify and manage project risks/gaps by clearly stating assumptions and working with clients/internal resources to mitigate risks in advance of project deployment.
  • Work with clients and internal resources to design, develop, document and execute on critical demonstrations/Proof of Concept's (POC's) of Evertz products and technologies
  • Ensure all documentation (drawings, quotes, requirement captures) are in place to hand the project over to Deployment Teams once the opportunity has been won
  • Travel as required to client sites

Qualifications:

  • Education in Electronics/Electrical/Computers/Television Broadcasting/Technical Copywriting or related field
  • Experience designing projects that have been implemented in a broadcast solution environment
  • Working knowledge of broadcast automation, device control, signal flow as well as media management and desk top workflow
  • Experience with Broadcast IT with network infrastructure an asset
  • Operational or Engineering experience working in a broadcast type facility
  • Knowledge of current digital video compression standards and file types
  • Excellent written and verbal communications skills
  • Ability to handle multiple priorities and manage deadlines in a fast paced sales environment
  • Experience with Jira, Confluence and Visio also an asset

title: Broadcast Engineer, Burbank, California
description:
We are seeking a skilled and motivated engineer to join our growing team. In this role, you will report to the Senior Manager of the Technical Operations Centre and play a key part in supporting and maintaining complex, cutting-edge broadcast video systems.

As a Broadcast Engineer, your primary responsibility will be to provide expert-level support for a range of advanced technologies, including software-defined video networking, video compression and content processing systems, orchestration platforms, and network management and monitoring tools.

This position offers a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of broadcast technology, collaborating with engineering teams and customers to ensure optimal system performance and rapid issue resolution.

Responsibilities:

  • Act as an expert level technical advisor or specialist on complex broadcast initiatives.
  • Perform system monitoring, verifying the integrity and availability of all broadcast hardware, server resources, systems and key processes, reviewing system and application logs.
  • Ensure events are managed until resolution while adhering to Incident and SLA guidelines.
  • Act as single point of contact for multiple support cases simultaneously.
  • Provide feedback on solution requirements, design specifications & operational guidelines.
  • Represent the department and coordinate with peer departments and Evertz broadcast customer to ensure effective delivery of project deliverables.
  • Establish necessary testing requirements to evaluate the proposed designs and to validate the technology platform or systems.
  • Document any departmental and technical procedures.

Qualifications:

  • Strong critical thinking skills.
  • Technical Diploma/Degree or equivalent broadcast experience (preferred).
  • Advanced knowledge of installations, upgrades and troubleshooting in Unix/Linux and Windows environments
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with many changing priorities.
  • Detail oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Self-motivated with the ability to take initiative in identifying and resolving problems independently.
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Willing to relocate to California; in order to provide long-term, on-site technical support to our customer.
  • Preference for candidates with working knowledge of one or more of the Evertz platforms.

What we Offer:

  • Competitive total compensation package, including benefits
  • Employer contribution to 401K
  • Relocation Assistance
  • Work-Life Balance
  • Career Progression 
  • Casual Work Environment 

Salary Range: Based on related experience and skill set.

title: Solutions Architect - Media/Broadcast Enterprise Systems
description:
Reporting to the Director of File Based Solutions, you work between Evertz clients, account managers, R&D engineers, project managers, and executive management to completely architect, design and document the requirements and implementation approach for Evertz end-to-end enterprise solutions for broadcast and media facilities.  The successful candidate must be able to capture our client’s vision, business requirement as well as technical requirements and map those into Evertz hardware, software and professional services offerings.  Candidate must be comfortable in both a client facing role as well as internally facing to R&D and delivery service groups.

Responsibilities:

  • Work collaboratively with the account manager to define and document the scope
  • Provide initial solutions to clients with Evertz equipment selection & systems design, refine over the design process to provide final quotation and supporting documentation
  • Work with internal resources and the client to provide client demonstrations and/or documentation as proof of concept system, as required
  • Work collaboratively with internal, client and third party teams to understand current and future business and technical requirements
  • Take lead on proposal creation and refinement, take ownership and produce accurate documentation for bid set.
  • Identify and manage project risks, clearly state assumptions, and work with client and internal resources to mitigate risks in advance of deployment.
  • Ultimately create a clear and well thought out design package, gap analysis, functional specifications, and budget
  • Identifying new customers and building relationships while maintaining the existing customer base
  • Act as the technical authority throughout the design, pre-commissioning, onsite commissioning, training and sustaining engineering phases
  • Travel as required to client sites
Qualifications:
  • Experience in designing, documenting and sustaining full life cycle end-to-end enterprise-wide client facing projects.
  • Experience working with file-based broadcast, production, post-production, non-linear distribution, automated transcoding and quality control, business/traffic systems type deployments and workflows i.e. the complete broadcast and media food chain.
  • Electronics engineering (or similar) education
  • Problem-solving, and strong communication skills
  • Results oriented, self-motivated, and team player
  • Familiar with BXF and industry standard traffic solutions
  • Must have 3 or more years experience deploying file based work flow solutions
  • Experience with Broadcast IT with network infrastructure
  • Understanding in applications integration with business systems via the use of XML as the interchange format
  • Knowledge of current digital video compression standards and file types
  • Good written and verbal communications skills

title: Project Manager
description:
This role manages the full lifecycle of project deployment, ensuring successful delivery of Evertz solutions. Responsibilities include gathering and approving functional specifications, developing project plans, coordinating pre-commissioning and logistics, and overseeing on-site and remote commissioning. The position ensures proper documentation, customer training, and timely issue resolution while providing regular status updates to keep projects on schedule. Acting as the main point of contact, the role supports all deployment stages and ensures customer satisfaction.

Responsibilities: 

  • Coordinating the capture, documentation and approval of the detailed requirements for the project ,(Functional Specification) working with the client team and the Evertz principal architect.
  • Development of the Evertz project plan, including the pre-deployment, production, logistics, commissioning, testing and acceptance phases.
  • Coordinating the pre-commissioning of all equipment with customer furnished design details (i.e IP addresses, frame layouts, labeling etc.).
  • Tracking production and shipping details of project equipment.
  • Coordination of the commissioning plan and Evertz resources for on-site and remote commissioning, confirming site readiness and access credentials for Evertz personnel (on- site and remote).
  •  Coordination project commissioning documentation and project acceptance.
  • Facilitating and ensuring proper training is provided to the customer on the usage of their new solution prior to the system hand-off (ie. operating, configuring and trouble-shooting of system).
  • Providing regular status reports and ensuring the project stays on track with the timeline.
  • Providing support and communication during all of the stages of deployment of project
  • Ensuring any issues that arise are dealt with and resolved in a timely manner and that all the customer’s needs are met.

Qualifications:

  • Education in Project Management or Electronics/Electrical/Computers/Broadcasting or related field
  • Project management certification such as PMP
  • Working experience with managing large scale complex technology development and deployment projects
  • Experience with Broadcast, IT and/or network infrastructure projects
  • Good written and verbal communications skills
  • Experience with Project Management tools and processes (i..e MS Project, SmartSheets  etc.)

title: Solutions Architect - Media/Broadcast Enterprise Systems
description:
Reporting to the Director of File Based Solutions, you work between Evertz clients, account managers, R&D engineers, project managers, and executive management to completely architect, design and document the requirements and implementation approach for Evertz end-to-end enterprise solutions for broadcast and media facilities.  The successful candidate must be able to capture our client’s vision, business requirement as well as technical requirements and map those into Evertz hardware, software and professional services offerings.  Candidate must be comfortable in both a client facing role as well as internally facing to R&D and delivery service groups.

Responsibilities:

  • Work collaboratively with the account manager to define and document the scope
  • Provide initial solutions to clients with Evertz equipment selection & systems design, refine over the design process to provide final quotation and supporting documentation
  • Work with internal resources and the client to provide client demonstrations and/or documentation as proof of concept system, as required
  • Work collaboratively with internal, client and third party teams to understand current and future business and technical requirements
  • Take lead on proposal creation and refinement, take ownership and produce accurate documentation for bid set.
  • Identify and manage project risks, clearly state assumptions, and work with client and internal resources to mitigate risks in advance of deployment.
  • Ultimately create a clear and well thought out design package, gap analysis, functional specifications, and budget
  • Identifying new customers and building relationships while maintaining the existing customer base
  • Act as the technical authority throughout the design, pre-commissioning, onsite commissioning, training and sustaining engineering phases
  • Travel as required to client sites
Qualifications:
  • Experience in designing, documenting and sustaining full life cycle end-to-end enterprise-wide client facing projects.
  • Experience working with file-based broadcast, production, post-production, non-linear distribution, automated transcoding and quality control, business/traffic systems type deployments and workflows i.e. the complete broadcast and media food chain.
  • Electronics engineering (or similar) education
  • Problem-solving, and strong communication skills
  • Results oriented, self-motivated, and team player
  • Familiar with BXF and industry standard traffic solutions
  • Must have 3 or more years experience deploying file based work flow solutions
  • Experience with Broadcast IT with network infrastructure
  • Understanding in applications integration with business systems via the use of XML as the interchange format
  • Knowledge of current digital video compression standards and file types
  • Good written and verbal communications skills

About Evertz:

Evertz Microsystems (TSX:ET) is a leading global manufacturer of broadcast equipment and solutions that deliver content to television sets, on-demand services, WebTV, IPTV, and mobile devices (like phones and tablets). Evertz has expertise in delivering complete end-to-end broadcast solutions for all aspects of broadcast production including content creation, content distribution and content delivery.

Considered as an innovator by their customers, Evertz delivers cutting edge solutions that are unmatched in the industry in both hardware and software. Evertz delivers products and solutions that can be found in major broadcast facilities on every continent. Evertz’ customer base also includes telcos, satellite, cable TV, and IPTV providers.

With over 2,000 employees, that include hardware and software engineers, Evertz is one of the leaders in the broadcast industry. Evertz has a global presence with offices located in: Canada, United States, United Kingdom, Germany, United Arab Emirates, India, Hong Kong, China, Singapore, and Australia. Evertz was named one of Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies, which recognizes excellence in Canadian-owned and Canadian-managed companies. Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies identifies Canadian corporate success through companies focused on their core vision, creating stakeholder value and excelling in the global economy.

Evertz makes certain there is an equal employment opportunity for all employees and applicants for employment, including persons with disabilities. In compliance with AODA, Evertz will strive to provide accommodation to persons with disabilities in the recruitment process upon request. If you are selected for an interview and you require accommodation due to a disability during the recruitment process, please notify Human Resources upon scheduling your interview.

Thank you for considering a career with Evertz!

title: Tax Accountant
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive tax professional to support the Company’s Canadian and U.S. tax compliance and reporting requirements. This role plays a critical part in the preparation and filing of corporate tax returns, SR&ED claims, and government remittances, while ensuring ongoing compliance with regulatory systems and deadlines. Working closely with the accounting team, external advisors, and the executive team, the successful candidate will contribute to accurate financial reporting, support tax audits, research evolving tax regulations, and assist with day-to-day accounting operations and special projects.

Responsibilities:

  • Complete the preparation of the Company’s Canadian corporate tax returns, including leading the preparation and review of annual SR&ED filings.

title: Technical Support Engineer - Linux and Korean Language Expert
description:
We are seeking a technically driven Enterprise Support Engineer with professional Korean language proficiency to join our Enterprise Support team. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy solving complex technical challenges while supporting global customers and partnering with internal teams worldwide.
You will gain hands-on experience with enterprise systems, infrastructure, and applications, working collaboratively with experienced engineers across support, development, and operations in a global broadcast environment.

About Evertz
Evertz India is the subsidy of Evertz Microsystems (TSX:ET) is a leading global manufacturer of broadcast equipment and solutions that deliver content to television sets, on-demand services, WebTV, IPTV, and mobile devices (like phones and tablets). Evertz has expertise in delivering complete end-to-end broadcast solutions for all aspects of broadcast production including content creation, content distribution and content delivery. Considered as an innovator by their customers, Evertz delivers cutting edge solutions that are unmatched in the industry in both hardware and software. Evertz delivers products and solutions that can be found in major broadcast facilities on every continent. Evertz’ customer base also includes telcos, satellite, cable TV, and IPTV providers. With over 2000 employees, that include hardware and software engineers, Evertz is one of the leaders in the broadcast industry. Evertz has a global presence with offices located in: Canada, United States, United Kingdom, Germany, United Arab Emirates, India, Hong Kong, China, Singapore, and Australia. Evertz was named one of Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies, which recognizes excellence in Canadian-owned and Canadian-managed companies. Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies identifies Canadian corporate success through companies focused on their core vision, creating stakeholder value and excelling in the global economy.

In This Role, You Will:
• Provide technical support for enterprise applications, servers, and infrastructure
• Troubleshoot issues related to Linux/Unix OS, databases, networking, and application servers
• Analyse complex technical issues, identify root causes, and implement effective solutions
• Collaborate with global development, operations, and product teams, including Korean speaking stakeholders, to resolve escalated issues
• Communicate technical findings, updates, and resolutions in Korean and English
• Document solutions and contribute to internal knowledge bases
• Support automation and troubleshooting using Python and/or JavaScript scripts

What You’ll Need for This Role

Key Qualification Requirements
• Professional proficiency in Korean (spoken) - mandatory
• Minimum 1+years of experience on Linux/Unix operating systems
• Working proficiency in English
• Knowledge of networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, firewalls, routing, troubleshooting)
• Hands-on experience with databases (SQL queries, troubleshooting, performance checks)
• Familiarity with application servers such as Tomcat, Nginx, Apache, or similar
• Scripting skills in Python and/or JavaScript
• Logical and structured approach to problem-solving and debugging
• Strong communication and collaboration skills

Nice to Have
• Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS
• Familiarity with APIs and integration troubleshooting
• Experience with log analysis and monitoring tools
• Basic knowledge of DevOps tools (Git, CI/CD pipelines, Docker)
• Prior experience supporting Korean customers or global teams

Who We’re Looking For
• A technically driven individual with strong Korean language proficiency
• Eager to learn new technologies and take initiative
• Strong team player with a customer-first mindset
• Detail oriented, with strong end-to-end systems awareness and calm execution during critical incidents.


Your Growth
At Evertz, we believe your career should be more than just a job it should be a path to growth and achievement. If you see an opportunity, we encourage you to seize it. Push boundaries, challenge your own limits, and pursue the goals you’ve always dreamed of.
We’ll equip you with the resources, learning programs, and mentorship you need to accelerate your success both internally and externally. Our commitment is to support you every step of the way.
The possibilities are endless because we’re aiming high. With ambitious plans to expand globally, drive revenue growth, and deliver the world’s most advanced broadcasting experiences, we’re looking for passionate individuals to join us on this exciting journey.

The perks, more than a job, this is a place where your talent is recognized and your well-being matters empowering you to do great work while still enjoying life beyond the office.
  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities.
  • Inhouse knowledge and product training
  • Exposure to global broadcast platforms
  • Performance-related bonuses.
  • Insurance and medical plans.
  • Employee friendly leave policy

Work Culture
We embrace a Remote/Hybrid work model, giving you the best of both worlds’ flexibility and collaboration. This approach fuels our core drivers of innovation: diversity, adaptability, and strong connections. You’ll join a dynamic, inclusive team that thrives on creativity and fresh ideas.
Ask our employees at Evertz what they love most, and you’ll hear one word repeated: culture. Here, you can bring your authentic self to work. Your unique background whether it’s your ethnicity, orientation, beliefs, age, identity, or abilities adds value and perspective to everything we do.
We welcome talent from all walks of life and anyone eager to help us achieve our vision. If you’re ready to align with our values and lead innovation forward, we’d love to hear from you.

Office Timings: 1:00 PM – 9:00 PM IST (may vary based on business needs)
Work Model: Remote/Hybrid
Office Location: Bangalore, India
Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Senior Software Engineer, Cloud Backend
description:
Skills and Experience you will bring:

  • 6+ years of professional experience with Python
  • 6+ years of experience with design, planning & implementation of APIs (REST, protobuf)
  • At least 4 years of exposure to AWS Cloud computing
  • A good eye for well-structured, modular and maintainable code
  • A passion for writing unit tests for your code
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to communicate clearly
  • Keen on participating in peer-reviewing code for technical accuracy, ensuring that technical standards are met

Additional skills and experience that will be useful:

  • Familiar with microservices architectures and patterns
  • Experience creating Serverless applications using event-driven architecture (AWS API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB, EventBridge, etc).
  • Experience with observability frameworks (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, Honeycomb) and understanding of the importance of observability in distributed systems
  • Experience working with NoSQL databases (DynamoDB, MongoDB) 
  • Familiar with CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, AWS CodePipeline)
  • Knowledge of major architecture styles and design patterns (message oriented, event based, etc.)
  • Experience building SaaS products in a multi-tenant environment
  • Familiar with integration and contract testing
  • Knowledge of networking
  • Experience with infrastructure-as-code in the cloud (CloudFormation, AWS CDK, Terraform).
  • Interest in working with machine learning to create smart solutions for the broadcast industry

Location: Burlington, Ontario
Hours: Monday - Friday, 40 hours.
Vacancy: This is an existing position. 
Salary Range: $100K CAD - $125K CAD

About the Role

The evertz.io Engineering Team builds next-generation systems for content management and distribution in the Media and Entertainment industry. Disney, NBCUniversal, Discovery, BBC, and many other content producers and publishers use our products and services to make the most of their file-based and live content for the least effort.

We work with high quality video in real-time and non-real-time scenarios across a wide range of cutting-edge tech. Specializations within the group span from low-level video manipulation and analysis, through back-end management and orchestration services, to web delivered UIs. There may also be opportunities for working as a member of the Scientific Computing Group who work in computer vision, data science and machine learning, taking experiments in Jupyter notebooks through to deployment in production. This makes for a challenging and rewarding engineering experience of continual learning and plenty of opportunity to explore different parts of the stack.

Our technology stack includes a serverless microservice architecture that capitalizes on the full breadth of AWS services with code written in Python, Rust and Java. Our UI uses the latest versions of Angular, Typescript and NgRx. Our CI/CD pipelines leverage AWS, Jenkins, Nexus, and Bazel in addition to our in-house release-management application to build and release 100’s of software components.

“Work is a thing you do, not a place you go”

We work in agile, low-bureaucracy, high-creativity, cross-functional teams spread across the world. It’s a highly creative work environment where we support your growth with opportunities for career progression, mentoring others and third-party education. The team is built on trust and is relaxed, open and welcoming to all, and there’s fun to be had with regular social events and sports teams.

As part of this role, you will be expected to:

  • Participating in design discussions with Head of Stream Automation, Product Owners and Solution Architects for adding new or improving existing features 
  • Taking a feature from scoping requirement to actual launch in our cloud platform 
  • Design & document requirements/features/API 
  • Close collaboration with UI & QA team
  • Collaborate with SRE team to improve system observability, incident response, and troubleshooting
  • Develop high quality, easily understandable, fully unit tested software satisfying Definition of Done including safe deployment tests
  • Understand and use Shift left strategy/push down Test Pyramid when testing the developed feature 
  • Engage with and involve QA team where an integration/end-to-end test is required 
  • Continuous learning to improve yourself and mentoring Junior Engineers 
  • Keen on participating in peer review of code for technical accuracy, ensuring technical standards are met

Salary & Benefits

We offer a competitive salary with annual performance-based bonus and stock option schemes. A pension plan; an employer funded health and medical plan; life insurance plan; long term disability coverage; paid time off; an employee assistance program; and a discount platform. The availability and specifics of these benefits vary by location, details of which will be provided during the hiring process.

title: Software Development Engineer in Test ( MABL , Selenium/Cypress )
description:
We are seeking a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) with strong automation and quality engineering skills. In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain automated test solutions, while also contributing to manual testing efforts when needed. You will collaborate closely with developers, DevOps, and product teams to ensure we deliver high-quality, reliable, and well-tested software.

So, who are we?
Evertz India is the subsidy of Evertz Microsystems (TSX:ET) is a leading global manufacturer of broadcast equipment and solutions that deliver content to television sets, on-demand services, WebTV, IPTV, and mobile devices. Evertz has expertise in delivering complete end-to-end broadcast solutions for all aspects of broadcast production including content creation, content distribution and content delivery.
Evertz delivers cutting edge solutions that are unmatched in the industry in both hardware and software. Evertz delivers products and solutions that can be found in major broadcast facilities on every continent. Evertz’ customer base also includes telcos, satellite, cable TV, and IPTV providers.
With over 2,000 employees, that include hardware and software engineers, Evertz is one of the leaders in the broadcast industry. Evertz has a global presence with offices located in: Canada, United States, United Kingdom, Germany, United Arab Emirates, India, Hong Kong, China, Singapore, and Australia. Evertz has manufacturing locations in Burlington, Ontario, Canada, and within the United States in Indiana, Pennsylvania and Chandler, Arizona. Evertz was named one of Canada's 50 Best Managed Companies, which recognizes excellence in Canadian-owned and Canadian-managed companies. Canada's 50 Best Managed Companies identifies Canadian corporate success through companies focused on their core vision, creating stakeholder value and excelling in the global economy.

What You’ll Do
  • Quality Strategy & Testing: Collaborate early with product and engineering teams to define requirements, acceptance criteria, and testing strategy, combining exploratory testing, AI-driven insights, and risk-based prioritization to prevent defects early.
  • Automation & Framework Development: Design, build, and maintain scalable automation frameworks using MABL, Playwright/Cypress/Selenium, including Python-based test suites, complex selectors, and integrations with AWS via APIs and WebSocket’s.
  • CI/CD & Mentorship: Embed automated testing into CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions), maintain pipeline-as-code, and mentor junior engineers while defining organization-wide best practices and the Testing Pyramid.
What you'll need for this role?  
Key Qualification Requirements
  • 3-4 years of experience as an QA Automation Engineer in automating testcases and building framework using:
      • MABL
      • Cypress/Selenium
      • Python/Playwright
  • Experience with Mabl or similar low-code/no-code test automation platforms (e.g., Testim, TestProject, Katalon, etc.)
  • Strong proficiency in JavaScript and Typescript / Python for test automation and scripting.
  • Experience with CI/CD tools such as Jenkins and GitHub Actions.
  • Exposure to testing microservices, APIs, or distributed systems.
  • Expertise in writing robust XPath for UI element identification.
  • Strong understanding of QA methodologies and STLC, with hands-on experience in functional, regression, smoke, sanity, and integration testing.
  • Experience with manual and automation testing, using tools like Jira, TestRail, or similar test management systems.
  • Hands-on experience with Git and version control best practices.
Additional Skills and Experience That Will Be A Plus
  • Familiarity with video technologies, including:
      • ffmpeg, mediainfo, ffprobe
      • Video formats, streaming protocols, codecs, and ancillary data.
  • Knowledge of cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
  • ISTQB certification (Nice to have)

Who we’re looking for?
  • A quality-first mindset – you care deeply about the reliability and user experience of the product.
  • A collaborative attitude – working across engineering, DevOps, and product teams to deliver the best outcome.
  • Curiosity and initiative – continuously learning and bringing forward ideas to improve automation, processes, and tooling.

Your Growth
At Evertz, we believe your career should be more than just a job it should be a path to growth and achievement. If you see an opportunity, we encourage you to seize it. Push boundaries, challenge your own limits, and pursue the goals you’ve always dreamed of.
We’ll equip you with the resources, learning programs, and mentorship you need to accelerate your success both internally and externally. Our commitment is to support you every step of the way.
The possibilities are endless because we’re aiming high. With ambitious plans to expand globally, drive revenue growth, and deliver the world’s most advanced broadcasting experiences, we’re looking for passionate individuals to join us on this exciting journey.
The perks It really is more than a job. We’ll recognize your talent and make sure that you can still have a life at work, and outside of it.
  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities.
  • Inhouse knowledge and product training
  • Exposure to global broadcast platforms
  • Performance related bonuses.
  • Insurance and medical plans.
  • Employee friendly leave policy

We offer a hybrid and remote work model that balances flexibility with meaningful collaboration, supporting our commitment to innovation, adaptability, diversity, and strong connections.
At Evertz, you’ll be part of a dynamic and inclusive team that values creativity, fresh ideas, and collaboration. We believe diverse perspectives strengthen our work and fuel innovation, and we are united by a shared vision to grow and succeed together.
Office Timing: 1pm to 9pm IST
Office Location: Manyata Tech Park, Bangalore, India
Work Model: Hybrid / Remote

Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Tier 2 Technical Support – evTOC
description:
Please submit your documents in English when applying for this position.

We’re looking for a technically skilled, service-oriented professional who strives for continuous self-improvement, with a natural curiosity and the ability to learn independently. The ideal candidate brings a positive, can-do attitude, builds strong relationships with both customers and team members, and is motivated to grow within the broadcast technology space. They take ownership of issues, proactively seek solutions.

Important note: 
The role is currently remote. To support the position, we are in the process of setting up an on-site lab to ensure direct access to our solutions. A transition to a hybrid working model is planned in the longer term; however, this change will take time and is expected to occur no sooner than 1–2 years.
 

What does it take to be a Broadcast Support Engineer:

  • Contributing to a mission-critical 24/7/365 environment, with potential for off-hours shifts and on-call duties
  • Providing remote support to major Evertz customers across various time zones
  • Solving problems by replicating user workflows and identifying effective solutions
  • Documenting departmental and technical procedures 

What you will learn:

  • How to act as a bridge between customers and our product development teams
  • How to serve as an expert-level specialist on highly complex technical issues
  • How to collaborate across multiple teams (engineering, QA, product) in a fast-paced, multinational environment
  • How to troubleshoot real-world scenarios involving software, network, and system-level issues
  • How to maintain customer satisfaction while handling escalations and technical challenges

Responsibilities:

  • Respond and provide expert technical direction to customers regarding operational issues, firmware upgrades and general system maintenance.
  • Regularly assess customer applications and troubleshoot/diagnose issues through research and/or issue replication to determine the root cause.
  • Recommend solutions utilizing various levels of technical language appropriate to the customer in a timely, clear, and professional manner.
  • Maintain a communication link between customer service and other Evertz departments by partnering to resolve customer issues and communicating status/progress to customers.
  • Developing and setting up tools by generating or authoring customer guides and providing detailed technical information to the technical writing group.
  • Participate in on-call duty.
Requirements:
To join our growing evTOC team you’ll need the following to make our team better:   
  • At least 2 years of experience in technical or customer support roles
  • Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills
  • Experience with Linux (any flavour)
  • Problem-solving attitude and ability to react to changing situations
  • Ability to act effectively in high-pressure situations
  • Dedicated to delivering excellent customer service
  • Passionate about technology and eager to learn new software and hardware products
  • Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment
  • College diploma in computer science, engineering, networking, or a related field
  • Willingness to travel to customer sites as needed– typically up to 10% per year
  • Openness to attend internal training at our HQ in Canada 
Additional qualifications:
  • Python and JavaScript familiarity is preferred
  • Enthusiastic about Computer hardware
  • Understanding of network technologies (DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, Firewalls, Switches)
  • Willingness to learn and work with the most advanced solutions in the broadcast industry

Recruitment process is simple:

  1. Screening with recruiter (45 min)
  2. Technical interview with Hiring Manager and Technical Lead (60 min) 

About Us:

Evertz Microsystems (TSX:ET) is a leading global manufacturer of broadcast equipment and solutions that deliver content to television sets, on-demand services, WebTV, IPTV, and mobile devices (like phones and tablets). Evertz has expertise in delivering complete end-to-end broadcast solutions for all aspects of broadcast production including content creation, content distribution and content delivery.

Considered as an innovator by their customers, Evertz delivers cutting edge solutions that are unmatched in the industry in both hardware and software. Evertz delivers products and solutions that can be found in major broadcast facilities on every continent. Evertz’ customer base also includes telcos, satellite, cable TV, and IPTV providers.

We work in agile, low-bureaucracy, cross-functional teams spread across the world. It’s a highly creative work environment where the team is built on trust and is relaxed, open and welcoming to all. 

Evertz has engineering offices in Canada, England, Scotland, India, and now it's time for Poland!

title: Systems Technologist, India Office
description:
We are looking for a driven and detail-oriented System Technologist to join our growing team. In this role, you will be at the heart of our system operations ensuring performance, scalability, and security through comprehensive testing and hands-on technical support. You will collaborate with customers, troubleshoot complex issues, and play a key role in delivering reliable, high-quality solutions.

About Evertz
Evertz India is the subsidy of Evertz Microsystems (TSX:ET) is a leading global manufacturer of broadcast equipment and solutions that deliver content to television sets, on-demand services, WebTV, IPTV, and mobile devices (like phones and tablets). Evertz has expertise in delivering complete end-to-end broadcast solutions for all aspects of broadcast production including content creation, content distribution and content delivery. Considered as an innovator by their customers, Evertz delivers cutting edge solutions that are unmatched in the industry in both hardware and software. Evertz delivers products and solutions that can be found in major broadcast facilities on every continent. Evertz’ customer base also includes telcos, satellite, cable TV, and IPTV providers. With over 2000 employees, that include hardware and software engineers, Evertz is one of the leaders in the broadcast industry. Evertz has a global presence with offices located in: Canada, United States, United Kingdom, Germany, United Arab Emirates, India, Hong Kong, China, Singapore, and Australia. Evertz was named one of Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies, which recognizes excellence in Canadian-owned and Canadian-managed companies. Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies identifies Canadian corporate success through companies focused on their core vision, creating stakeholder value and excelling in the global economy.

Key Responsibilities
  • Perform functional, configuration, scalability, and security testing
  • Lead system level regression testing, and analyze pre and post upgrade results
  • Identify risks, gaps, and areas for improvement across the system
  • Provide Tier-2 support for product-related issues
  • Gather required information from customer support/reporters
  • Create, manage, and track Jira tickets through resolution
  • Identify root causes and escalate complex issues to the product team
  • Support customer systems remotely, including upgrade and rollback procedures
  • Maintain and enhance custom scripts and in-house automation tools
  • Contribute to ongoing test automation initiatives
  • Create clear, detailed technical documentation for system upgrades, fixes, and deployments
Required Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
  • 1+ year experience as a System Engineer or similar role
  • 1+ year experience in Linux/Unix environments (Debian or Ubuntu)
  • Working knowledge of virtualization (VMware or equivalent)
Skills That Give You an Edge
  • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP)
  • Knowledge of databases (MySQL / NoSQL)
  • Good understanding of networking fundamentals
  • 1+ year experience in Python or Bash scripting
Why Join Us
  • Work on cutting-edge systems and technologies
  • Gain hands-on experience across testing, automation, and support
  • Collaborate with a dynamic, solution-focused team
  • Make a real impact on product quality and customer experience

Your Growth
At Evertz, we believe a career should be more than just a job, it should be a journey of growth, learning, and achievement. We provide mentorship, resources, and opportunities to help you explore your potential, take on challenges, and reach your goals. With ambitious plans to expand globally and deliver cutting-edge broadcasting experiences, you will be part of a team where your talent is valued, your contributions matter, and your well-being is supported every step of the way.
A place where you are empowered to do great work and can benefit from,
  • Flexible working hours and work from home opportunities
  • Inhouse knowledge and product training
  • Exposure to global broadcast platforms
  • Performance-related bonuses.
  • Insurance and medical plans.
  • Employee friendly leave policy

Work Culture
We offer a Remote/Hybrid work model that balances flexibility with collaboration, fueling innovation through diversity and adaptability.
At Evertz, one of our strengths is our culture, a place where you can bring your authentic self, and where diverse perspectives and experiences are valued. We welcome talent from all backgrounds who are eager to contribute, grow, and help us drive innovation forward.

Office Timings: 1:00 PM – 9:00 PM IST (may vary based on business needs)
Work Model: Remote/Hybrid
Office Location: Bangalore, India
Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!
